incorrects.description: Student selected comparing fiction to nonfiction, skipping the combined step

params.description: Nonfiction and Poetry bars highlight together.

steps.text: The question asks about nonfiction and poetry combined. When you see combined, find that total first.

incorrects.description: Student selected adding all categories, not focusing on the needed categories

steps.text: We don't need all categories. The question only asks about nonfiction and poetry combined. Start with those two.

incorrects.description: Student selected subtracting poetry from nonfiction, incorrect operation for combined

params.description: Nonfiction and Poetry bars highlight together.

steps.text: Combined means add, not subtract. We need the total of nonfiction and poetry together.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct first step.

params.description: Nonfiction and Poetry bars highlight. Calculation shows: 30 + 20 = 50.

steps.text: When we see combined, we add first. Nonfiction is 30, poetry is 20. That's 50 combined. The answer is to add nonfiction and poetry.

incorrects.description: Student selected 10, subtracted values instead of adding

params.description: Nonfiction and Poetry bars pulse to emphasize adding.

steps.text: Combined means add them together, not subtract. Nonfiction is 30, poetry is 20. What's 30 plus 20?

incorrects.description: Student selected 40, may have confused with Comics value or miscounted

params.description: Nonfiction and Poetry bars highlight. Values 30 and 20 emphasized.

steps.text: Look at nonfiction: 30. Look at poetry: 20. Add those two together. What do you get?

incorrects.description: Student selected 60, may have added extra categories or misread values

params.description: Only Nonfiction and Poetry bars highlighted.

steps.text: We only need these two categories. Nonfiction is 30, poetry is 20. Count just those two bars.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct calculation.

params.description: Nonfiction bar (30) and Poetry bar (20) highlight. Calculation appears: 30 + 20 = 50.

steps.text: Nonfiction is 30 books. Poetry is 20 books. When we add them, 30 plus 20 equals 50. The combined total is 50.

incorrects.description: Student selected 100, added instead of subtracting

params.description: Fiction bar highlights alongside displayed value 50.

steps.text: How many more means compare. Fiction is 50, combined is 50. Are they the same or different?

incorrects.description: Student selected 50, stated one value instead of finding the difference

params.description: Fiction bar highlights. Combined value 50 displayed next to it.

steps.text: 50 is the fiction value, but the question asks how many more. Subtract: 50 minus 50. What's the difference?

incorrects.description: Student selected 10, may have miscalculated or compared wrong categories

params.description: Fiction bar highlights. Combined value 50 displayed.

steps.text: Fiction is 50. Combined is 50. When two amounts are equal, the difference is zero, not 10.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the comparison.

params.description: Fiction bar (50) and combined value (50) appear side by side. Calculation shows: 50 - 50 = 0.

steps.text: Fiction is 50 books. Nonfiction and poetry combined is also 50. When we compare equal amounts, 50 minus 50 equals 0. There is no difference. The answer is 0.
